agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer and entity money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML/sanctions screening), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services environment where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.",
    "bounded_context": "Wallet Transfers & Account Compliance",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "KYC (Know Your Customer)", "definition": "Identity verification process that a user must complete before being granted full transactional capabilities (send, withdraw). Governs the transition from unverified to verified status." },
      { "term": "Verification Tier", "definition": "The user's current identity verification level: unverified (receive only), partially-verified (send up to $500/day), or fully-verified (send up to $5,000/day)." },
      { "term": "Daily Transfer Limit", "definition": "The maximum aggregate outbound transfer amount permitted within a rolling or calendar day, determined by the user's verification tier." },
      { "term": "Settlement", "definition": "The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation), after which the transaction becomes irreversible." },
      { "term": "Sanctioned Entity", "definition": "Any individual, organization, or jurisdiction appearing on applicable sanctions lists (e.g., OFAC, EU, UN). Transfers to these entities must be blocked before execution." },
      { "term": "Account Lock", "definition": "A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. A locked account cannot perform any actions until a support agent manually unlocks it." },
      { "term": "Transaction Notification", "definition": "An alert sent to the account holder for any transaction exceeding $1,000 in value." },
      { "term": "T+1", "definition": "Settlement timing convention meaning one business day after the transaction is initiated." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A user must complete KYC identity verification before they are permitted to send money or withdraw funds.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Regulatory requirement. Allowing an unverified user to send money violates KYC/AML regulations and exposes the platform to legal liability. This is not a policy preference — it is a compliance gate.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to initiate a send or withdrawal by a user whose verification status is 'unverified' MUST be rejected with an appropriate error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Unverified accounts may receive money but cannot send or withdraw.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Defines the minimum capability set for unverified accounts. Receiving is permitted to allow onboarding flows (e.g., someone sends money to a new user), but outbound movement of funds without identity verification would violate regulatory requirements.",
      "testable_assertion": "An inbound transfer to an unverified account MUST succeed. An outbound transfer or withdrawal from an unverified account MUST be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Fully-verified accounts have a daily transfer limit of $5,000. Partially-verified accounts have a daily transfer limit of $500.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Dollar thresholds are business policy decisions that can be adjusted by product or compliance leadership. The concept of having limits is invariant, but the specific dollar amounts are configurable policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "A fully-verified user attempting to send an amount that would cause their daily aggregate to exceed $5,000 MUST be rejected. A partially-verified user attempting to send an amount that would cause their daily aggregate to exceed $500 MUST be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Settlement finality is a fundamental financial integrity constraint. Reversing settled transactions would corrupt ledger state, break accounting reconciliation, and potentially violate payment network rules. Pre-settlement cancellation may be possible, but post-settlement reversal is prohibited.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any reversal request for a transaction whose status is 'settled' MUST be rejected. Only transactions in a pre-settlement state MAY be eligible for cancellation."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Legal/regulatory mandate. Processing a transfer to a sanctioned entity is a criminal offense in most jurisdictions. This is not a soft preference — failure to block is a compliance violation with severe legal consequences.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any transfer where the recipient matches a sanctioned entity MUST be blocked before funds move. The transaction MUST NOT reach a pending or settled state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Five consecutive failed login attempts must lock the account. Only a support agent can unlock a locked account.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The threshold of 5 attempts and the unlock mechanism (support agent only) are security policy decisions. A VP of security could reasonably change the threshold to 3 or 10, or add self-service unlock via secondary authentication. The concept of lockout exists to prevent brute-force attacks, but the parameters are policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account MUST transition to 'locked' state. A locked account MUST reject all login attempts regardless of correct credentials. Only a support agent action MUST be able to transition the account back to 'unlocked'."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "A notification must be sent for any transaction exceeding $1,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $1,000 threshold is a product/compliance policy decision. The notification mechanism and threshold could be changed without corrupting system state. Note: some jurisdictions require reporting at certain thresholds (e.g., $10,000 CTR in the US), but $1,000 appears to be a product-level alert, not a regulatory filing.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is processed, a notification MUST be dispatched to the account holder. Transactions of exactly $1,000 or below MUST NOT trigger this notification."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A user with verification status 'unverified'",
      "when": "The user attempts to send money to another user",
      "then": "The transfer is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A user with verification status 'unverified'",
      "when": "Another user sends money to their account",
      "then": "The transfer succeeds and the funds are credited to the unverified user's balance"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A fully-verified user who has already sent $4,800 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $300",
      "then": "The transfer is rejected because the daily aggregate ($5,100) would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A partially-verified user who has sent $0 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $501",
      "then": "The transfer is rejected because it exceeds the $500 daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A transaction that was initiated yesterday and has reached 'settled' status (T+1)",
      "when": "A reversal is requested for that transaction",
      "then": "The reversal is rejected with an error indicating settled transactions cannot be reversed"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A transaction that was initiated today and is still in 'pending' (pre-settlement) status",
      "when": "A cancellation is requested for that transaction",
      "then": "The system permits cancellation (spec does not explicitly forbid pre-settlement cancellation)"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A verified user initiating a transfer to a recipient",
      "when": "The recipient matches an entity on the sanctions list",
      "then": "The transfer is blocked before any funds move, and the transaction is flagged for compliance review"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A user who has failed login 4 times consecutively",
      "when": "The user fails login a 5th consecutive time",
      "then": "The account is locked and all subsequent login attempts are rejected, even with correct credentials"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A locked account",
      "when": "A support agent initiates an unlock action",
      "then": "The account is unlocked and the user can log in with correct credentials; the failed attempt counter is reset"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A user who has failed login 3 times, then succeeds on the 4th attempt",
      "when": "The user subsequently fails 4 more times",
      "then": "The account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure count was reset by the successful login"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A verified user sending a transaction of $1,500",
      "when": "The transaction is processed successfully",
      "then": "A notification is sent to the account holder informing them of the transaction"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A verified user sending a transaction of exactly $1,000",
      "when": "The transaction is processed successfully",
      "then": "No notification is triggered (the rule specifies 'over $1,000', not 'at or over')"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "An unverified user with a balance",
      "when": "The user attempts to withdraw funds",
      "then": "The withdrawal is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A partially-verified user attempts to send exactly $500", "expected_behavior": "The transfer should succeed — $500 is at the limit, not over it. However, the spec says 'limits are $500' which is ambiguous about whether the limit is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend treating it as inclusive (up to and including $500)." },
    { "scenario": "A user receives money that pushes their balance above the daily send limit", "expected_behavior": "Receiving is unrestricted by daily limits. Limits apply only to outbound transfers." },
    { "scenario": "A transaction of exactly $1,000 — does it trigger a notification?", "expected_behavior": "No. The spec says 'over $1,000' which means strictly greater than. $1,000.00 exactly does not trigger. $1,000.01 does." },
    { "scenario": "A sanctions list is updated after a transfer is initiated but before settlement", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address this. Recommended: re-screen at settlement time. If the recipient is now sanctioned, block settlement and escalate to compliance." },
    { "scenario": "A locked account receives an inbound transfer", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address this. Account lock is described as a login security measure. Inbound transfers should likely still be credited since they don't require the locked user's authentication." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple transactions initiated simultaneously that individually are within the daily limit but collectively exceed it", "expected_behavior": "The daily limit must be enforced atomically. If two $3,000 transfers are submitted concurrently by a verified user, only one should succeed. Race conditions here would violate the daily limit invariant." },
    { "scenario": "A user completes KYC verification mid-day after already receiving funds", "expected_behavior": "Upon verification, the user gains send/withdraw capability. Their daily transfer limit counter should start fresh for the day (they had no outbound activity while unverified)." },
    { "scenario": "Does the $1,000 notification apply to receiving as well as sending?", "expected_behavior": "Spec says 'any transaction over $1,000' — this likely includes both inbound and outbound. However, this is ambiguous and should be clarified with the PM." },
    { "scenario": "A support agent unlocks the account — does the failed attempt counter reset?", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not explicitly state. The counter should reset to zero on unlock; otherwise the user could be locked out again after a single failed attempt." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Daily transfer limits cap platform throughput and risk exposure. Incorrect limit enforcement could result in fraud losses or regulatory fines. Settlement finality (T+1) affects cash flow and float management. Sanctions violations carry fines potentially in the millions.",
    "operational": "Account lockout mechanism will generate support ticket volume — every locked account requires a manual support agent interaction. Sanctions screening must integrate with an up-to-date sanctions list provider and handle latency. KYC verification pipeline must be reliable to avoid blocking legitimate users from transacting.",
    "user": "Unverified users have a restricted experience (receive-only), creating friction but also an incentive to complete KYC. Account lockout after 5 attempts may frustrate legitimate users who forget passwords. The $1,000 notification threshold provides transaction visibility and security confidence.",
    "risk": "Primary risks: (1) Sanctions screening failure — legal/criminal liability; (2) Daily limit bypass via race conditions — fraud exposure; (3) KYC gate bypass — regulatory violation; (4) Settlement reversal bug — ledger corruption and financial loss. All invariant rules carry severe consequences if violated.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "End Users (Wallet Holders)", "impact": "Tiered experience based on verification status. Security lockout can temporarily block access. Notifications improve trust and transaction visibility." },
      { "group": "Compliance / Legal Team", "impact": "Directly accountable for KYC and sanctions enforcement. Must be confident that sanctions screening is comprehensive and audit-logged." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Account lockout unlocks are a manual workload driver. Must have tooling to unlock accounts and verify user identity before doing so." },
      { "group": "Finance / Treasury", "impact": "Settlement finality affects reconciliation. Daily limits affect transaction volume and revenue from fees." },
      { "group": "Product / Growth", "impact": "KYC friction affects conversion funnel. Partially-verified tier ($500 limit) may serve as a conversion step." },
      { "group": "Engineering / Security", "impact": "Must implement atomic daily limit enforcement, real-time sanctions screening, and brute-force protection. Race conditions in limit checks are a critical engineering concern." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec is reasonably clear on the core rules but has several ambiguities: (1) whether daily limits are inclusive or exclusive of the stated amount, (2) whether notifications apply to inbound transactions, (3) what happens to locked accounts receiving inbound transfers, (4) whether pre-settlement cancellation is permitted, (5) what 'daily' means (calendar day in which timezone, or rolling 24h), (6) no mention of currency handling or multi-currency scenarios. The regulatory rules (KYC, sanctions) are clear in intent but lack implementation specificity (which sanctions lists, KYC verification levels).",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "KYC gate for send/withdraw is clear and invariant",
      "Settlement finality at T+1 is unambiguous",
      "Sanctions blocking requirement is clear",
      "Account lockout trigger (5 consecutive failures) and unlock mechanism (support agent) are explicit"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether daily limit boundary values are inclusive ($500 exactly) or exclusive",
      "Whether $1,000 notification threshold applies to receiving transactions or only sending",
      "Definition of 'daily' — calendar day (which timezone?) or rolling 24-hour window",
      "Pre-settlement cancellation policy — spec only says settled cannot be reversed, silent on pending",
      "Behavior of locked accounts for non-login operations (receiving funds, scheduled transfers)",
      "Whether partially-verified users can withdraw (spec only mentions send limits for partial verification)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Daily transfer limit is inclusive — a $500 transfer by a partially-verified user is permitted",
      "'Daily' refers to a calendar day, timezone TBD",
      "Notification for 'any transaction over $1,000' means strictly greater than $1,000.00",
      "Account lock only prevents login; inbound transfers to locked accounts still credit",
      "The consecutive failed login counter resets on successful login",
      "Partially-verified users can send (up to $500/day) but the spec is ambiguous on whether they can withdraw"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec does not define what 'daily' means for daily transfer limits — is it a calendar day (and in which timezone: user's local, UTC, platform HQ?) or a rolling 24-hour window? This fundamentally affects limit enforcement and user experience.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Engineering", "Compliance"],
      "options": ["Calendar day in UTC", "Calendar day in user's local timezone", "Rolling 24-hour window from first transaction"],
      "recommendation": "Calendar day in UTC for simplicity and consistency, with clear communication to users."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The $1,000 notification rule says 'any transaction over $1,000' — does this include inbound (received) transactions, or only outbound (sent/withdrawn)? The word 'any' suggests both, but the business intent may differ.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Product"],
      "options": ["Notify on all transactions (send and receive) over $1,000", "Notify only on outbound transactions over $1,000"],
      "recommendation": "Notify on all transactions over $1,000 — this is a security feature and users benefit from knowing about large inbound transfers too."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Can partially-verified users withdraw, or only send? The spec states partially-verified accounts have a $500 daily transfer limit but only explicitly restricts unverified accounts from withdrawal. It's unclear if 'transfer limit' encompasses withdrawals.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Compliance", "Product"],
      "options": ["Partially-verified can withdraw up to their daily limit", "Partially-verified cannot withdraw (same as unverified for withdrawals)"],
      "recommendation": "Partially-verified should be able to withdraw within their $500 daily limit, since the spec only fully restricts unverified accounts from withdrawal."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No currency or multi-currency specification. Are daily limits in USD only? What happens if a user sends in EUR — is the limit converted at the spot rate? Are all wallets single-currency?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Finance", "Engineering"],
      "options": ["Single-currency (USD) platform", "Multi-currency with USD-equivalent limits"],
      "recommendation": "Clarify with PM. If multi-currency, daily limits should be enforced against USD-equivalent values using a defined exchange rate source."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
